label: Ear canal irritation after swimming without otitis externa or foreign body red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated ear canal irritation after swimming.
No emergency red flags, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Red flags requiring a separate ED pathway, unstable vital signs, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, ENT, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: cdc.swimmers_ear, medlineplus.swimmers_ear supports patient-education framing for ear canal irritation after swimming and boundary design. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only when clinician diagnosis is documented and red flags requiring a separate pathway are absent.
